visual_description: A man in glasses and a suit is shown in close-up against a dark background. Two men are seated side by side in a dimly lit room with blinds visible behind them; one wears a white shirt and tie, the other a denim shirt. The man in the denim shirt looks forward with his arms crossed, while the man in the white shirt gazes slightly to the side.
audio_description: The audio clip opens with a brief, low-level electronic hiss, indicating a quiet, controlled indoor environment. A single male voice, speaking in Cantonese, begins with a calm and measured tone: “我多謝你合作。” (“I thank you for your cooperation.”) His delivery is formal and polite, with a slight downward inflection at the end, suggesting a routine acknowledgment. The voice is clear and close to the microphone, with no background noise or reverberation, and the recording is of high fidelity, capturing both low and high frequencies without distortion. After a short pause, the same speaker continues, his tone shifting to one of gentle reproach: “你生你講嘅咁隱晦，冇人明。” (“You were born to speak so obscurely, no one understands.”) This statement is delivered with a subtle rise and fall in pitch. The phrase is a colloquial, culturally specific complaint, implying that the other party’s communication is unnecessarily vague or difficult to comprehend.
